concerns.text: T025 instructs 'DO NOT use Paired Wilcoxon' but fails to mandate the specific alternative verification method required by FR-009. FR-009 explicitly requires 'Bootstrap Confidence Intervals (95% CI, 10,000 resamples)' to compare mean R²/RMSE. The task description omits the mandatory '10,000 resamples' and the specific 'Bootstrap CI' mechanism, creating a semantic gap where an implementer could choose an unapproved statistical method.

T022 requires verifying the sign aligns with 'electrostatics' generically. FR-013 explicitly mandates a 'Physical Plausibility Check' where the sign of the coefficient for the top feature MUST align with first-principles electrostatics (specifically: 'positive charge density correlates with increased anion affinity'). The task description drops the specific physics model (charge density/affinity relationship) required by the spec, weakening the validation constraint.

T026 requires reporting the analysis as 'underpowered' if N < 10. SC-006 and FR-012 require that if N < 10, the system MUST report the CI width as 'wide' (a specific output format/string) in addition to the underpowered status. The task omits the specific 'wide' flag requirement, risking a deliverable that fails the success criteria's formatting constraint.

T012 describes the simulated data formula but omits the mandatory step in FR-011 to '(2) Identify the most abundant halide in the available data' before generation. FR-011 explicitly lists this as a required sub-step of the fallback logic. The task description implies the formula is sufficient, silently dropping a required data-processing step.
